title: Liberal Political Institutions Index
subtitle: Data by [V-Dem](#dod:v-dem). Expert estimates of the extent of individual and minority rights, equality before the law, and constraints on the executive by the legislature and courts. The index ranges from 0 to 1 (most liberal).
caption:
Liberal Political Institutions Index

Data by V-Dem. Expert estimates of the extent of individual and minority rights, equality before the law, and constraints on the executive by the legislature and courts. The index ranges from 0 to 1 (most liberal).

This choropleth world map displays the Liberal Political Institutions Index for 2025, with a color scale ranging from pale yellow (0) to dark blue (1). The legend includes tick marks at intervals of 0.1. The darkest blue shades, representing values between 0.8 and 1.0, are visible in Canada, the United States, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and most of Western and Northern Europe. In South America, Argentina and Uruguay appear in dark blue bands, while Brazil and Colombia sit in mid-range teal shades between 0.4 and 0.6. Across Africa, South Africa and Botswana fall into darker blue bands above 0.6, while a broad swath of Central and West African countries appear in pale yellow and light green shades below 0.3. In Asia, India and Indonesia sit in mid-range teal bands, while China and Russia appear in pale yellow shades near the bottom of the scale. The Middle East shows predominantly light yellow and pale green colors, with most countries falling below 0.4. Greenland displays a hatched pattern indicating no data. Eastern European countries show variation, with some appearing in mid-range shades and others in lighter bands. The visual pattern shows the highest index values concentrated in North America, Western Europe, and Oceania, with the lowest values distributed across parts of Africa, the Middle East, and Central Asia.
